steps.title: Substitute x equals 2 into the function
steps.content:
We need to find $f(2)$ by substituting $x = 2$ into $f(x) = 5x^3 + 3$:

$$f(2) = 5(2)^3 + 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate and simplify
steps.content:
Calculate the exponent: $2^3 = 8$. Then multiply by 5 and add 3:

$$f(2) = 5(8) + 3 = 40 + 3 = 43$$

The value of $f(2)$ is 43.
